title: Micropholis mensalis
taxonomy: Micropholis mensalis is classified in the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapodilla family. This family consists of over 1,000 species, including trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. Micropholis mensalis is a species of fruit-bearing tree found in South America. It belongs to the genus Micropholis, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The classification of Micropholis mensalis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Sapotaceae family.

intro: Micropholis mensalis is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, commonly known as a type of sapote tree native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Micropholis mensalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
